channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Moss (YC F25) Is Hiring
item.description: Article URL: https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/moss/jobs/52LnqLQ-software-engineer-sdk Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48865332 Points: 0 # Comments: 0

item.title: Weekly Dev Log 2026-W13
item.description: 🗓️ This Week This week, I mainly focused on iOS development📱. I also tested a small workflow using Codex: organizing ideas, creating a design file, and implementing a SwiftUI prototype. Through...

item.title: Putting "Doing Nothing" on Your Schedule (Bite-size Article)
item.description: Introduction When we plan our days, we usually write down what we're going to do. Meetings, errands, exercise, study. On the other hand, we rarely put "doing nothing" on the schedule. I'm...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: “Squidbleed” Vulnerability
item.description: In a rare combined cybersecurity/squid post, a twenty-nine-year-old squid proxy bug can leak HTTP requests. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the...

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
item.description: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAi's senior leadership, including a long-time former employee.

item.title: How We Test an AI Product Without Burning Credit
item.description: Most tests are cheap. You click a button, you assert something changed, you run it a thousand times and nobody notices. Testing an AI product is different, because the interesting behaviour comes...

item.title: GPT-5.6, Grok 4.5, Claude, and Muse Spark build the same 4 apps
item.description: Article URL: https://www.tryai.dev/blog/gpt-5.6-build-off-12-models Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48865093 Points: 12 # Comments: 2

item.title: From Prompt Files to Agent Skills: How I Unified My Content Automation
item.description: A few weeks ago I wrote about how I use AI agents and MCP to automate my website's content. That post covered the why — I create a lot of content and keeping my site up to date was tedious. This...

item.title: I built an open-source internship tracker that polls 3,593 company career feeds
item.description: You always miss an internship because you find out about it when its too late... So I built something so it never happens again. As a computer science student, the hardest part of recruiting isn't...

item.title: I Built an AI That Calls Candidates and Scores Them So Recruiters Don't Have To
item.description: The first-round phone screen is the most boring part of hiring. A recruiter calls a candidate. Asks the same five questions. Takes notes. Decides whether to advance them. Does it again. And again....

item.title: Apple Is Suing OpenAI for Allegedly Stealing Hardware Secrets
item.description: The iPhone-maker claims OpenAI encouraged poached employees to bring over confidential presentations, secret prototypes, and key supplier details.

item.title: GPT-5.6 Sol tops the frontend leaderboard
item.description: GPT-5.6 Sol tops the frontend leaderboard Fresh metrics from Arena AI delivered an unexpected result: GPT-5.6 Sol xHigh shares first place with Claude Fable 5 in Code Arena: Frontend. This is the...

item.title: I Built an 8-bit RPG With Zero Image or Audio Files. Here's How.
item.description: A game with no assets folder Open the source of most browser games and you will find a public/ directory sagging under the weight of PNGs, sprite sheets, and .mp3 files. Open the source of Pixel...

item.title: A decade later, Pokémon Go finally made good on its original promise
item.description: When Niantic dropped the first Pokémon Go trailer in 2015, it was hard to grasp how a bunch of players could work together to catch a pokémon like Mewtwo. But this week at the game's 10th...

item.title: ICE is threatening to deport witnesses of its latest shooting
item.description: Advocates are demanding that the Department of Homeland Security release bodycam footage of the fatal shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican immigrant who was killed by ICE officers in...

item.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
item.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open...

item.title: Nvidia’s biggest RAM supplier just had a trillion-dollar debut on Wall Street
item.description: As the AI boom boosts demand for RAM, SK Hynix - one of the world's biggest suppliers of memory chips - launched on Wall Street Friday. The South Korean chipmaker opened at $170 per share and...

item.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
item.description: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

item.title: Spotify will let you fine-tune your weekly Release Radar playlist
item.description: Spotify is giving listeners control to fine-tune what gets surfaced for them in Release Radar - one of its most popular weekly playlists. The new options allow you to narrow the playlist to a...

item.title: URGENT - Progress Tells ShareFile Customers to Shut Down Storage Zone Controllers Over Security Threat
item.description: Progress Software has told ShareFile customers to shut down the Windows servers running their Storage Zone Controllers, confirming to The Hacker News that it is responding to a "credible external...

item.title: Netflix is turning into YouTube
item.description: Netflix has shows and movies. And video games. And live sports. And podcasts. And also, apparently, YouTube videos? For a company that used to seem like the next big thing in TV, it all feels a...

item.title: Injective Labs GitHub Compromise Pushes Wallet-Key-Stealing npm Packages
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and leveraged it to publish a malicious package on the npm registry to steal cryptocurrency wallet private keys...

item.title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
item.description: Researchers at firmware security firm Binarly have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips...

item.title: Disney Plus is reportedly looking into a free streaming tier
item.description: Disney Plus is considering making some of its content free to watch, according to a report from Business Insider. A source tells the outlet that Adam Smith, Disney's chief product and technology...

item.title: Anker’s 3-in-1 Qi2.2 charging station is $95 off
item.description: If you’re looking to declutter your nightstand and quickly charge up to three of your most-used gadgets, Anker’s Prime Wireless Charging Station is an easy way to do both. The station has spots...

item.title: Laser Attack Resets Tangem Wallet Passwords on Cards That Can't Be Patched
item.description: Researchers at Ledger's Donjon security team have shown that a precisely timed laser pulse, aimed at the chip inside a Tangem crypto wallet card, can reset the card's password to anything the...

item.title: I spent a week using the Trump phone — it sucks
item.description: The Trump phone was never a serious phone. Not when it was announced last June, in dodgy renders and with an incoherent spec sheet. Nor when Trump Mobile admitted - just two weeks later - that it...

item.title: Researcher Details WhatsApp-to-Host Attack Chain Using Three OpenClaw Flaws
item.description: Details have emerged about three now-patched security flaws in the OpenClaw personal artificial intelligence (AI) assistant that, if successfully exploited, could enable credential theft,...

item.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
item.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open...

item.title: I’m filling in at The Verge for 6 weeks. Ask me anything!
item.description: Hello my friends! My protocol pals! My computational compadres! I'm David, a technology reporter and cohost of the Waveform Podcast. I'll be hosting a subscriber-only "AMA" today at 11AM PT / 2PM...
